Miller sat at his desk, the afternoon sunlight stretching shadows across the cluttered mess. He lifted the receiver, dialed Vance's number with quick taps. After a few rings, Vance picked up, impatience crackling through the line. 

Miller leaned forward, his shoulders tense. “Vance, get the code transferred by the end of the day,” he demanded. He could almost picture Vance's brow furrowing in frustration. 

“Yeah, I got it,” Vance said. His distracted tone suggested he was preoccupied. “I’ll handle it.” 

Miller's grip tightened on the receiver, the plastic cold against his palm. “I’m counting on you,” he pressed, voice steady.
